x4freetext: 300-700 keV alpha beams from the JN accelerator at
the CASPAR underground laboratory. Beam intensities
of He+ ions ranged between 60-70 microA.
moderator houses two concentric rings of 3He
proportional counters, containing a total of 16
detectors. The polyethylene moderates neutrons to
thermal energies, which are then detected through the
electrical signal induced by the 3He(n,p)3H reaction.
used. These targets were created by vacuum

Ta backing. The target thicknesses were determined by

Pulse shape discrimination was used to mitigate
the intrinsic alpha activity of the detectors using
the rise time discrimination method. The pulse shape
background of the detectors, while preserving 35% of
the neutron signal. Yields from the 11B(alpha,n) 14N
reaction were measured using a 4pi neutron detector.
Yield data were converted to cross sections as
sigma(Eeff) = Yield(E0)/(f*nx), where nx is the
number of active target nuclei, Y is the yield at
beam energy E0, and Eeff is the effective energy and
